name: Reuters World News
bias_note: Owned by Thomson Reuters. Widely regarded as the international standard for factual wire reporting. Minimal editorial bias.
usage_notes: Public RSS deprecated — feeds.reuters.com DNS is dead as of 2024. API access requires a Reuters commercial licensing agreement. Disabled until API credentials are obtained.

name: Associated Press — Top News
bias_note: Non-profit cooperative owned by member newspapers. Strong tradition of editorial independence.
usage_notes: Public RSS removed — apnews.com/apf-topnews returns HTML as of 2024. AP Datafeeds API requires commercial licensing. Disabled until API credentials are obtained.

name: BBC News — World
bias_note: UK public broadcaster funded by licence fee. Strong editorial guidelines. Occasional criticism of Western-centric framing on Global South stories.
usage_notes: Feed is stable and well-maintained. Topic-specific feeds also available, e.g. /news/world/middle_east/rss.xml.

name: Deutsche Welle — World
bias_note: German state international broadcaster. Editorially independent from the German government per its charter. Strong Africa and Eastern Europe coverage.
usage_notes: DW RSS feeds are stable. Additional language feeds available at rss.dw.com.

name: Al Jazeera English
bias_note: Funded by the Qatari government. Strong on Global South and MENA coverage that Western outlets under-report. May reflect Qatari foreign policy positions on regional disputes.
usage_notes: RSS feed is stable and frequently updated. Valuable for non-Western perspective on MENA and African events.

name: France 24 — English
bias_note: French public international broadcaster. Strong Africa coverage reflecting French regional interests. Some alignment with French foreign policy positions.
usage_notes: Verify RSS URL — France24 has reorganized feeds. Try /en/frnewsfeed as fallback if /en/rss returns empty.

name: The New York Times — World
bias_note: Privately owned. Center-left editorial stance on US domestic issues; international reporting is generally factual and well-resourced.
usage_notes: Feed headers contain full metadata. Article body links hit a paywall. Treat titles, descriptions, and dates as the usable content unit.

name: The Washington Post — World
bias_note: Owned by Jeff Bezos. Center-left editorial stance. Generally independent international desk.
usage_notes: RSS includes headlines and summaries. Full articles paywalled. Feed freshness is good.

name: Bloomberg — Markets & Finance
bias_note: Primarily financial data and markets. Strong energy and commodities coverage. Financially sophisticated readership focus.
usage_notes: Bloomberg RSS is largely paywalled. Feed metadata (headline, date) is accessible. Valuable for market-moving headlines even without full body access.

name: NPR — News
bias_note: US public radio. Center-left perceived bias on US domestic coverage. International reporting is generally straightforward.
usage_notes: NPR RSS feeds are stable and include full article summaries. One of the few major English-language outlets with fully open article content.

name: Xinhua — English World News
bias_note: Official state news agency of the PRC. Reflects CCP positions on all topics. Essential for understanding Beijing's official narrative, not an independent source.
usage_notes: xinhuanet.com RSS infrastructure was decommissioned — worldrss.xml serves 2017–2018 articles only. english.news.cn replacement domain has no working RSS endpoints (all return 404). Disabled until a live feed is confirmed.

name: CGTN — China Global Television Network
bias_note: State-owned international broadcaster under CCP control. Banned from broadcasting in the UK (2021) for lack of editorial independence. Valuable for tracking official Chinese international messaging.
usage_notes: Section XML feeds confirmed working at /subscribe/rss/section/{world,china,politics,...}.xml. World section last updated April 13 2026 — all articles stale (outside 30-day window) as of May 2026. Enable when feed resumes regular updates.

name: South China Morning Post
bias_note: Owned by Alibaba Group since 2016. Maintains editorial independence for most coverage but may soften reporting on Alibaba or CCP-sensitive topics. Best English-language source for nuanced China analysis.
usage_notes: Feed ID 91 covers international news. SCMP provides section-specific RSS feeds. Some articles behind soft paywall — headlines and summaries accessible.

name: Global Times
bias_note: Published by People's Daily, the CCP's official organ. More aggressive in tone than Xinhua. Often used to float nationalist positions or test international reactions. Essential for tracking hardline CCP rhetoric.
usage_notes: Feed is publicly accessible and free. Use alongside Xinhua and CGTN to triangulate official Chinese narrative positioning.

name: Bangkok Post — Top Stories
bias_note: Oldest English-language newspaper in Thailand. Generally independent but operates within Thai press freedom constraints. Practices self-censorship on monarchy-related topics due to lèse-majesté law.
usage_notes: Feed is stable and well-structured. Section-specific feeds also available: /rss/data/news.xml, /rss/data/business.xml. Full articles are open access.

name: The Nation Thailand
bias_note: English-language Thai daily. Historically seen as pro-establishment. Subject to same lèse-majesté self-censorship pressures as Bangkok Post.
usage_notes: RSS feed not found as of May 2026. /rss/news returns HTML, /feed redirects to an article listing page. The Nation redesigned their site post-2019 ownership change and may have dropped RSS. Disabled until a working feed URL is confirmed.

name: Khaosod English
bias_note: English edition of Khaosod, a mid-market Thai-language paper. More politically critical than Bangkok Post. Has covered protest movements and human rights cases.
usage_notes: Standard WordPress RSS feed — reliable and well-formed. Fully open access. Smaller team means lower posting frequency than Bangkok Post.

name: Thai PBS World
bias_note: English service of Thai Public Broadcasting Service. Funded by excise tax on alcohol and tobacco — not directly government-controlled, but operates within Thai political environment.
usage_notes: Site migrated from thaipbsworld.com to world.thaipbs.or.th — original URL now redirects. New domain /feed/ returns HTML (text/html) not XML as of May 2026, suggesting a non-WordPress CMS that does not expose a standard RSS feed. Disabled until a valid feed endpoint is found.

name: Matichon
bias_note: One of Thailand's most-read Thai-language newspapers. Center-left readership. More willing than English-language peers to cover political opposition and social movements.
usage_notes: Thai-language content — requires machine translation before event extraction. Disabled by default until the reporter-agent has translation capability.
